purpose: SIGGRAPH paper demo candidates for text-to-Genesis generation and tuning.
selection_rules: No cloth, fluid, or RL/control-heavy examples.
Prefer complete paper demo scenes over isolated stress tests.
Keep the suite balanced across pure rigid, soft with rigid boundaries, and rigid-soft two-way coupling.

examples.title: anchor_chain_windup
examples.prompt: Create a pure rigid-body scene where a heavy anchor hangs from a chain of interlocking rigid links, drops under gravity, then a motorized horizontal winch axle winds the chain so links bunch and kink while lifting the anchor, render 10s behavior.
examples.notes: A complete rigid chain-and-winch demo; no deformable bodies, fluids, cloth, or learned control.

examples.title: bike_chain_sprocket
examples.prompt: Create a pure rigid-body scene with a loop of interlocking chain links around two toothed sprockets; motorize one sprocket so the chain wraps, slides, and drives the other sprocket through tight contact, render 10s behavior.
examples.notes: A contact-rich rigid mechanism demo with a simple motorized sprocket control.

examples.title: expanding_lock_box
examples.prompt: Create a pure rigid-body mechanism scene of an expanding lock box: a gear-like key turns a central spiral cam, retracts five sliding locking pins through contact, then the bottom panel drops open under gravity, render 10s behavior.
examples.notes: A complete rigid mechanical demo and a good long-term target for generated articulated/mesh assets.

examples.title: halloween_pumpkin_party
examples.paper: Penetration-free Projective Dynamics on the GPU
examples.prompt: Create a stylized soft-body Halloween scene with soft pumpkin and ghost toy meshes bouncing around a rigid twisted tree and ground props, colliding and settling with no fabric or fluids, render 10s behavior.
examples.notes: A complete stylized soft-body scene. It relies on generated toy/prop meshes but no cloth or fluid simulation.

examples.title: soft_dragon_obstacle_course
examples.paper: Penetration-free Projective Dynamics on the GPU
examples.prompt: Create a soft-body scene where a long soft dragon toy is released through a field of fixed vertical rigid posts, bending, twisting, and tumbling around the obstacles through contact, render 10s behavior.
examples.notes: A non-cloth soft mesh demo with static rigid obstacle contact.

examples.title: soft_toys_squeeze_through_tube
examples.paper: Incremental Potential Contact: Intersection- and Inversion-Free, Large-Deformation Dynamics
examples.prompt: Create a rigid-soft two-way coupled squeeze-out scene inside a fixed transparent container whose upper part is a vertical cylindrical tube and whose lower part is a tapered conical funnel with a small flat-cut circular opening at the very bottom. Pack the container with several soft FEM toy meshes: a red soft ball, a green lizard or dinosaur, a blue spider or crab-like toy, a tan bumpy plush animal, and a purple teardrop toy. A heavy dynamic rigid cylindrical plunger descends from above and physically presses the toys downward; every toy including the ball visibly squashes, bulges, jams against other toys and the transparent wall, squeezes through the small bottom opening, rebounds, and separates into a soft pile below, render 10s behavior.
examples.notes: A complete IPC squeeze-out demo adapted to two-way coupling by making the plunger a physical rigid participant.

examples.title: spiky_soft_balls_pinball
examples.paper: Second-Order Stencil Descent for Interior-Point Hyperelasticity
examples.prompt: Create a soft-body pinball-like scene with several spiky soft balls inside a narrow arena of fixed rigid side walls and staggered short rigid pegs; launch or drop the balls so they bounce, snag, deform, and settle around the pegs, render 10s behavior.
examples.notes: A non-cloth soft-body contact scene with repeated obstacle impacts.

examples.title: woven_basket_soft_impact
examples.paper: Second-Order Stencil Descent for Interior-Point Hyperelasticity
examples.prompt: Create a rigid-soft coupled scene where a deformable woven basket made from elastic soft ring or lattice elements supports rigid branch-like objects and soft foliage; the objects fall into the basket, deform it deeply, and settle together through two-way contact, render 10s behavior.
examples.notes: A more complete mixed rigid/deformable demo without fabric. Approximate the woven structure with generated soft lattice or soft torus/ring elements.
